content.full_text:
California 
I 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
August 28, 1989 
Ginger Bremberg 
Glendale city council 
613 E. Broadway, Room 300 
Glendale, CA 
91206-4391 
Dear Councilwoman Bremberg: 
Re: 
Your request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-445 
This is in reply to your letter requesting advice concerning 
your reporting obligations under the conflict of interest 
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l 
QUESTION 
Are you required to disclose as a gift, a charitable donation 
made in your name? 
If so, is the gift subject to the gift and 
honorarium limitation in Government Code Section 85400? 
CONCLUSION 
The donation, in your name, to the National Lupus 
Erythematosus Foundation is not considered a gift to you. 
FACTS 
You received notification from the National Lupus 
Erythematosus Foundation that Assemblyman Tom Bane made a donation 
to the foundation in your name to congratulate you on your 
reappointmen~ to the California Waste Management Board. 
The 
amount of the donation has not been disclosed to you. 
1Government Code Section 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660 


Ginger Bremberg 
Page Two 
ANALYSIS 
Government Code section 82028 defines a gift as" 
any 
payment to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value 
is not received ... " 
Elected officials must disclose gifts 
received from a single source aggregating $50 or more in a 
calendar year. (Section 87207.) 
Regulation 18726.1 (copy enclosed) states that: 
... a gift is "provided to, received by, or promised to" an 
official at the time the offer is extended if the gift is 
offered to the official in such a manner that it is within 
the official's discretion whether or not to accept it and the 
official ultimately decides to accept the gift. 
A gift is 
deemed accepted if it is tendered and comes under the control 
of the recipient official ... 
Because you had no discretion in whether to accept the 
donation to the National Lupus Erythematosus Foundation, and the 
donation did not come under your control, you have not received a 
reportable gift. 
In addition, please note that the $1,000 gift and honorarium 
limitation in Government Code section 85400 relates only to gifts 
and honoraria received in connection with a speech, article or 
published work on a subject relating to the governmental process. 
Commission Regulation 18540(d) (copy enclosed) defines 
governmental process as "activities related to the official duties 
of the elected officer, or the functions, programs and policies of 
government." 
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please 
contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
G~ni?dk 
By: Colleen McGee 
Political Reform Consultant 
enclosures 


CITY OF Glendale CALIFORNIA 
Office of the 
CITY COUNCIL 
613 E. Broadway, Room 200, Glendale, California 91206-4391 
• 
July 21, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 90804 
(818) 956-4844 
I 
am 
in receipt of a 
card from the National Lupus 
Erythematosus 
Founda tion 
informing 
me 
of 
"a 
contribution" 
made 
to it 
in 
my 
name. 
The 
said 
contribution was made by the Honorable Tom Bane but the 
amount is not stated. 
The contribution is alleged to 
have been made "to congratulate" me on my reappointment 
to the California Waste Management Board. 
In view of the fact that I am not only a member of the 
California Waste Management Board, but also an elected 
member to the Glendale City Council, and a member of the 
Glendale Redevelopment Agency, I am concerned with the 
provisions of Proposition 73 as this gift being a gift 
from a single source as defined in your regulations. 
Not knowing the amount of the gift, did I receive a gift 
which 
must 
be 
reported under the provisions of the 
Poli tical Reform Act? 
To what extent am I responsible 
to determine the amount of the gift made on my behalf? 
Having never met the gentleman, I am curious as to why 
the contribution was made. 
Your 
formal 
written advise 
is 
requested under 
the 
provisions of Government Code S. 84114(d). 
::..:l truly yours,

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 28, 1989 
Honorable Ginger Bremberg 
Councilmember 
613 E. Broadway, Room 200 
Glendale, CA 
91206-4391 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-445 
Dear Ms. Bremberg: 
Your letter requesting advice under the political Reform Act 
was received on July 26, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, _ 
~eanne Pritchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

QUESTION: Are you required to disclose as a gift, a charitable donation 
made in your name? 
If so, is the gift subject to the gift and 
honorarium limitation in Government Code Section 85400?

CONCLUSION: The donation, in your name, to the National Lupus 
Erythematosus Foundation is not considered a gift to you.
